FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class Virtual

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

Fluent factories for virtualized collections. All recycle over the slab free-list and keep a 0-alloc in-window scroll. Custom(int, IVirtualLayout, Func<int, Element>, Func<int, string>?, int, bool) takes any IVirtualLayout you implement. The returned VirtualListEl record lives in FluentGpu.Reconciler (the reconciler diffs it directly).

public static class Virtual

Custom(int, IVirtualLayout, Func<int, Element>, Func<int, string>?, int, bool)

A virtualized collection with ANY custom IVirtualLayout you supply.

public static VirtualListEl Custom(int itemCount, IVirtualLayout layout, Func<int, Element> renderItem, Func<int, string>? keyOf = null, int overscan = 4, bool horizontal = false)

Grid(int, int, float, float, Func<int, Element>, Func<int, string>?, int)

A vertically-virtualized uniform card GRID (album/artist shelves) — virtualizes by row.

public static VirtualListEl Grid(int itemCount, int columns, float itemHeight, float gap, Func<int, Element> renderItem, Func<int, string>? keyOf = null, int overscan = 2)

GridBound(int, int, float, float, Func<IReadSignal<int>, Element>, int)

Signals-first BOUND uniform card grid — ListBound(int, float, Func<IReadSignal<int>, Element>, int) semantics over GridVirtualLayout.

public static VirtualListEl GridBound(int itemCount, int columns, float itemHeight, float gap, Func<IReadSignal<int>, Element> row, int overscan = 2)

Parameters

itemCount int
columns int
itemHeight float
gap float
row Func<IReadSignal<int>, Element>
overscan int

Returns

VirtualListEl

GroupedList(int, int[], float, float, Func<int, Element>, out GroupedListVirtualLayout, Func<int, string>?, int)

A grouped flat list with measured rows + sticky-header hook (E11-L0 grouping): headers occupy flat indices of their own (headerIndices, sorted ascending) and are rendered by the same renderItem — a group header is just a measured item KIND. Keep a reference to the GroupedListVirtualLayout (via layout) for StickyHeaderIndexAt.

public static VirtualListEl GroupedList(int itemCount, int[] headerIndices, float headerExtent, float itemEstimate, Func<int, Element> renderItem, out GroupedListVirtualLayout layout, Func<int, string>? keyOf = null, int overscan = 4)

Parameters

itemCount int
headerIndices int[]
headerExtent float
itemEstimate float
renderItem Func<int, Element>
layout GroupedListVirtualLayout
keyOf Func<int, string>
overscan int

Returns

VirtualListEl

HorizontalGrid(int, int, float, float, Func<int, Element>, Func<int, string>?, int)

A HORIZONTALLY-scrolling uniform card grid (a shelf rows cells tall) — the horizontal variant of Grid(int, int, float, float, Func<int, Element>, Func<int, string>?, int); virtualizes by column.

public static VirtualListEl HorizontalGrid(int itemCount, int rows, float itemWidth, float gap, Func<int, Element> renderItem, Func<int, string>? keyOf = null, int overscan = 2)

Parameters

itemCount int
rows int
itemWidth float
gap float
renderItem Func<int, Element>
keyOf Func<int, string>
overscan int

Returns

VirtualListEl

LinedFlow(int, float, Func<int, Element>, Func<int, float>?, float, float, Func<int, string>?, int)

The WinUI LinedFlowLayout photo-wall (ItemsView's signature layout): uniform-height lines, per-item width = aspect × lineHeight, wrapping at the viewport edge. The layout instance is stateful — reuse the returned element's Layout or hoist your own LinedFlowLayout.

public static VirtualListEl LinedFlow(int itemCount, float lineHeight, Func<int, Element> renderItem, Func<int, float>? aspectRatio = null, float lineSpacing = 0, float minItemSpacing = 0, Func<int, string>? keyOf = null, int overscan = 8)

Parameters

itemCount int
lineHeight float
renderItem Func<int, Element>
aspectRatio Func<int, float>
lineSpacing float
minItemSpacing float
keyOf Func<int, string>
overscan int

Returns

VirtualListEl

List(int, float, Func<int, Element>, Func<int, string>?, int)

A vertically-virtualized uniform list (the WaveeMusic track-list shape).

public static VirtualListEl List(int itemCount, float itemExtent, Func<int, Element> renderItem, Func<int, string>? keyOf = null, int overscan = 4)

Parameters

itemCount int
itemExtent float
renderItem Func<int, Element>
keyOf Func<int, string>
overscan int

Returns

VirtualListEl

ListBound(int, float, Func<IReadSignal<int>, Element>, int)

Signals-first BOUND list (the recycler fast path): row runs ONCE per visible slot with an index SIGNAL; scrolling rebinds a slot by writing its signal — zero element rebuild, zero reconcile, zero keys. Express anything that varies by index as a reactive bind (TextBind/FillBind/ SourceBind/PlaceholderBind), never a captured value. The fastest path for huge uniform lists (the WaveeMusic 100k track list under a scrollbar thumb-drag).

public static VirtualListEl ListBound(int itemCount, float itemExtent, Func<IReadSignal<int>, Element> row, int overscan = 4)

Parameters

itemCount int
itemExtent float
row Func<IReadSignal<int>, Element>
overscan int

Returns

VirtualListEl

Measured(int, IMeasuredVirtualLayout, Func<int, Element>, Func<int, string>?, int, bool)

A virtualized collection over ANY variable-extent IMeasuredVirtualLayout (E11-L0): rows realize at the layout's estimate, correct to their measured extent on arrange, and the engine re-pins the scroll anchor across corrections. The layout is STATEFUL — create it once (hoist in a UseMemo) and reuse it across renders.

public static VirtualListEl Measured(int itemCount, IMeasuredVirtualLayout layout, Func<int, Element> renderItem, Func<int, string>? keyOf = null, int overscan = 4, bool horizontal = false)

Parameters

itemCount int
layout IMeasuredVirtualLayout
renderItem Func<int, Element>
keyOf Func<int, string>
overscan int
horizontal bool

Returns

VirtualListEl

SpanGrid(int, int, float, float, Func<int, int>, Func<int, Element>, Func<int, string>?, int)

Uniform-row grid with ITEM SPANNING (hero-as-first-row): spanOf returns each item's column span (clamped 1..columns); items pack row-major and wrap when a span doesn't fit.

public static VirtualListEl SpanGrid(int itemCount, int columns, float rowHeight, float gap, Func<int, int> spanOf, Func<int, Element> renderItem, Func<int, string>? keyOf = null, int overscan = 2)

Parameters

itemCount int
columns int
rowHeight float
gap float
spanOf Func<int, int>
renderItem Func<int, Element>
keyOf Func<int, string>
overscan int

Returns

VirtualListEl

VariableList(int, float, Func<int, Element>, Func<int, string>?, int)

A vertically-virtualized list of variable-height rows (Fenwick extent table + scroll anchoring) — the legacy built-in variable path (Layout = null). The seam-shaped equivalent is Measured(int, IMeasuredVirtualLayout, Func<int, Element>, Func<int, string>?, int, bool) with a MeasuredStackVirtualLayout (E11-L0), which custom layouts mirror.

public static VirtualListEl VariableList(int itemCount, float estimatedExtent, Func<int, Element> renderItem, Func<int, string>? keyOf = null, int overscan = 4)

Parameters

itemCount int
estimatedExtent float
renderItem Func<int, Element>
keyOf Func<int, string>
overscan int

Returns

VirtualListEl
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
